description: Supportive reframing language used throughout TRUE AI to reduce shame, normalize human complexity, and encourage sustainable self-awareness.

overload.reframe: Overload is often a signal that too much has been carried for too long, not proof of personal failure.
Emotional exhaustion can emerge gradually when responsibility consistently outweighs recovery.
Difficulty sustaining a certain pace does not necessarily mean you are incapable. It may simply mean the pace itself is unsustainable.
Rest is not the opposite of growth. Recovery is part of sustainability.

uncertainty.reframe: You do not need complete clarity in order to begin moving more intentionally.
Periods of uncertainty often emerge during moments when older patterns no longer feel fully aligned.
Conflicting needs can coexist. Wanting both stability and change is deeply human.
Sustainable movement is often shaped through gradual adjustment rather than perfect certainty.

boundaries.reframe: Boundaries are not rejection. They are often an attempt to preserve sustainability.
Care for others becomes more difficult to sustain when personal capacity is consistently ignored.
Protecting your capacity does not erase your care for other people.
Some responsibilities begin as meaningful contribution and gradually become difficult to sustain without adjustment.

self_worth.reframe: Your value is not limited to what you produce, manage, or sustain for other people.
Growth rarely follows a perfectly linear timeline.
Struggling with capacity or direction does not erase the strengths you already possess.

growth.reframe: Meaningful movement does not always require dramatic reinvention.
Sustainable growth is often quieter and slower than people expect.
Temporary difficulty does not erase long-term movement.
Small sustainable adjustments can still create meaningful change over time.

identity.reframe: Identity can evolve gradually as values, capacity, and priorities shift over time.

identity.trigger: participant feels trapped in old roles
identity.reframe: Roles that once felt aligned may eventually require reevaluation or adjustment.
Constant performance can create distance from emotional steadiness and authenticity.
You are allowed to outgrow patterns that no longer feel sustainable or meaningful.

emotional_complexity.reframe: Multiple emotional truths can exist at the same time.
Not every emotional experience needs immediate resolution or interpretation.
Human needs and emotional states naturally shift across seasons of life.
Complex emotions are not necessarily signs of confusion or weakness.
